other_observers: None; Christian Nunes found the bird about 9am. Bill Kaempfer, David Waltman and Todd Deininger also saw it.
description: This was a small loon; about the same size as nearby Western Grebes, but with a shorter neck and a more substantial body. The crown down to the eyeline was a darkish gray; the rest of the head and neck were light but mottled. The rest of the visible bird was also gray and with some mottling on the back. The bill was a light color. The bird was sleeping quite a bit, but when it raised its head, it immediately went into the position where the bill is raised about 10 degrees above the horizontal. This is the same way that a D-c Cormorant holds its bill.  D-c Cormorants were also on Baseline Res, but this bird was distinctly smaller and differently shaped.Behaviors: The bird was sleeping quite a bit, but raised its head three or four times while I watched it. I did not see it dive; it always remained on the surface.Call: None HeardPlumage: First Winter
